title: Handle play failures gracefully
description: Define real failure conditions and recovery so a mid-run error does not leave hosts half-converged.
body: Steps: 1. Identify tasks whose nonzero exit does not mean real failure and set failed_when accordingly. 2. Wrap risky sequences in block/rescue/always to define recovery. 3. Decide on any_errors_fatal versus per-host continuation for the play. 4. Ensure handlers needed for a safe state can still flush. 5. Test a forced failure mid-play and inspect the resulting host state. Rollback point: before enabling the play against more hosts. Validation: an injected failure leaves each host in a defined, recoverable state, not a partial one.
